briefdescription.para: Define XSecure_Sha3Hash with XSecure_Sha384Hash to maintain backward compatibility.
This function initializes a XSecure_Sha3 structure with the default values required for operating the SHA3 cryptographic engine.

listitem.para: Error out if the dma type is invalid.

briefdescription.para: This function configures Secure Stream Switch and starts the SHA-3 engine.

listitem.para: XSECURE_SHA3_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Sha3 *InstancePtr, const u64 InDataAddr, const u32 Size)
memberdef.name: XSecure_Sha3Update

briefdescription.para: This function updates the SHA3 engine with the input data.

parameterdescription.para: Starting address of the data which has to be updated to SHA engine
Size of the input data in bytes

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If the update is successful
XSECURE_SHA3_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred
XST_FAILURE - If there is a failure in SSS configuration
Update input data to SHA3 engine

briefdescription.para: This function updates the SHA3 engine with the input data located at a 64-bit address.

parameterdescription.para: Starting 64 bit address of the data which has to be updated to SHA engine
Size of the input data in bytes

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If the update is successful
XSECURE_SHA3_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred
XST_FAILURE - If there is a failure in SSS configuration
Update input data to SHA3 engine
Set SHA under reset on failure condition

briefdescription.para: This function updates SHA3 engine with final data which includes SHA3 padding and reads final hash on complete data.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XSecure_Sha3Hash structure, where output hash is stored into Hash which is a member of XSecure_Sha3Hash structure

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If finished without any errors
XSECURE_SHA3_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred
XST_FAILURE - If Sha3PadType is other than KECCAK or NIST
Perform SHA3 padding based on IsLastUpdate flag
Read out the Hash in reverse order.

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Sha3 *InstancePtr, const u64 InDataAddr, const u32 Size, XSecure_Sha3Hash *Sha3Hash)
memberdef.name: XSecure_Sha3Digest

briefdescription.para: This function calculates the SHA-3 digest on the given input data.

parameterdescription.para: Starting address of the data on which sha3 hash should be calculated
Pointer to XSecure_Sha3Hash structure, where output hash is stored into Hash which is a member of XSecure_Sha3Hash structure

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If digest calculation done successfully
XST_FAILURE - If any error from Sha3Update or Sha3Finish
Perform padding and read out the hash

briefdescription.para: This function reads the SHA3 hash of the data and it can be called between calls to XSecure_Sha3Update.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to XSecure_Sha3Hash structure, where output hash is stored into Hash which is a member of XSecure_Sha3Hash structure

listitem.para: XSECURE_SHA3_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred
Read the hash from the registers and store in the local buffer in reverse order

briefdescription.para: This function notifies the SHA driver at the end of the SHA data update and last update includes padding also.

detaileddescription.para: This function is called before XSecure_Sha3Finish to prevent driver adding the padding.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If last update can be accepted
XSECURE_SHA3_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred

briefdescription.para: This function assigns SHA3 configuration to the InstancePtr.
This function initializes a XSecure_Sha3 structure with the default values required for operating the SHA3 cryptographic engine.
This function configures Secure Stream Switch and starts the SHA-3 engine.

listitem.para: XSECURE_SHA3_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred

briefdescription.para: This function updates the SHA3 engine with the input data located at a 64-bit address.

parameterdescription.para: Starting 64 bit address of the data which has to be updated to SHA engine
Size of the input data in bytes

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If the update is successful
XSECURE_SHA3_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred
XST_FAILURE - If there is a failure in SSS configuration

briefdescription.para: This function updates SHA3 engine with final data which includes SHA3 padding and reads final hash on complete data.

parameterdescription.para: Address of XSecure_Sha3Hash structure, where output hash is stored into Hash which is a member of XSecure_Sha3Hash structure
Size of the hash in bytes

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If finished without any errors
XSECURE_SHA3_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred
XST_FAILURE - If Sha3PadType is other than KECCAK or NIST

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Sha *InstancePtr, XSecure_ShaMode ShaMode, const u64 DataAddr, const u32 DataSize, u64 HashAddr, const u32 HashBufSize)
memberdef.name: XSecure_ShaDigest

briefdescription.para: This wrapper function calls SHA3 digest on the given input data.

parameterdescription.para: Starting address of the data on which sha3 hash should be calculated
Address of XSecure_Sha3Hash structure, where output hash is stored into Hash which is a member of XSecure_Sha3Hash structure
Size of the hash in bytes

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If digest calculation done successfully
XST_FAILURE - If any error from Sha3Update or Sha3Finish

briefdescription.para: This function notifies the SHA driver at the end of the SHA data update and last update includes padding also.

detaileddescription.para: Typically called before XSecure_Sha3Finish to prevent driver adding the padding.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If last update can be accepted
XSECURE_SHA3_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred

briefdescription.para: This function assigns SHA configuration to the InstancePtr.
This file Contains the function prototypes, defines and macros for the SHA-384 hardware module.

detaileddescription.para: This driver supports the following features:
The SHA-3 driver instance can be initialized in the following way:
A pointer to XPmcDma instance has to be passed in initialization as PMC DMA will be used for data transfers to SHA module.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 har 03/20/20 Initial release 4.2 har 03/20/20 Updated file version to sync with library version bvi 04/07/20 Renamed csudma as pmcdma 4.3 ana 06/04/20 created XSecure_Sha3Hash structure variable kpt 08/26/20 Changed argument type from u8* to UINTPTR rpo 09/10/20 Changed the return type of some prototypes am 09/24/20 Resolved MISRA C violations har 10/12/20 Addressed security review comments ana 10/15/20 Updated doxygen tags 4.5 bm 01/13/21 Added support for 64-bit input data address 5.0 bm 07/06/22 Refactor versal and versal_net code kpt 07/24/22 Moved XSecure_Sha3Kat into xsecure_kat.c dc 08/26/22 Changed u8 to u32 type for size optimization 5.1 mmd 07/09/23 Included header file for crypto algorithm information vss 07/14/23 Added IsResourceBusy and IpiMask variables in Xsecure_Sha instance 5.4 yog 04/29/24 Fixed doxygen grouping and doxygen warnings. tri 10/16/24 Fixed redefined warning pre 03/02/25 Removed data context lost setting 5.7 mb 04/16/26 Define XSECURE_HASH_SIZE_IN_BYTES macro tbk 04/13/26 Remove unwanted header include
